procurement.What it covers: Higher-value public contract notices, tenders, awards and pipeline/planned-procurement notices (post Procurement Act 2023).
procurement.Notes & relevance to digital health: TIER 1 backbone. OCDS JSON API (release & record packages), v1.1.5. Dev docs at /Developer/Documentation. Fully automatable.

procurement.What it covers: Lower-value contracts (£12k+ central gov), pipeline/planned procurement, some award notices.
procurement.Notes & relevance to digital health: TIER 1 backbone. OCDS API + CSV export. Dev docs at /apidocumentation. Fully automatable.

procurement.What it covers: NHS tender notices, planned procurements and framework opportunities across the majority of NHS organisations.
procurement.Notes & relevance to digital health: TIER 3, closest thing to a single NHS portal (44k+ suppliers). No open API; free email business alerts. Monitor, do not scrape.

procurement.What it covers: National framework agreements, incl. the £900m (inc VAT) Healthcare AI Solutions framework SBS10523 (8 lots, submissions close 23 Jun 2026, runs via SAP Ariba).
procurement.Notes & relevance to digital health: TIER 3, the 'stealth release' problem source. ~40% of the NHS uses SBS. Watch frameworks page + cross-check FTS. High editorial value.

procurement.What it covers: Products & consumables; Spark DPS = innovative med-tech and remote monitoring, open-entry for SMEs.
procurement.Notes & relevance to digital health: Spark DPS is the SME-friendly innovation route, strongest for devices/remote monitoring, less so for pure software.

procurement.What it covers: Cross-government frameworks, several health-relevant; operates G-Cloud and Spark DPS.
procurement.Notes & relevance to digital health: Health sector landing page lists relevant agreements. Pipeline worth monitoring for upcoming digital frameworks.

procurement.What it covers: Cloud hosting, SaaS software and digital support, the primary procurement route for software-based digital health products.
procurement.Notes & relevance to digital health: Most relevant framework for digital health SaaS. Being listed = discoverable to NHS buyers. Track application windows.

procurement.What it covers: Population health management, advanced analytics, digital & service transformation suppliers.
procurement.Update cadence: Legacy, no new call-offs being initiated
procurement.Notes & relevance to digital health: NHS England has stopped initiating new HSSF call-offs; existing ones continue. A refresh/successor has been signalled, watch for it.

procurement.What it covers: NHS England's own procurement activity, supplier guidance and commercial policy.
procurement.Notes & relevance to digital health: Reference/guidance layer rather than live opportunities, useful for the 'how to contract' editorial content.

procurement.Source: NOE CPC (North of England Commercial Procurement Collaborative)
procurement.Geographic scope: England (frameworks open to all UK contracting authorities)
procurement.What it covers: Collaborative frameworks across many categories; Central Commercial Function accredited framework host.
procurement.Notes & relevance to digital health: TIER 3. Part of NHS Procurement in Partnership (NHS PiP), members access all four hubs' framework portfolios.

procurement.What it covers: Collaborative NHS frameworks and procurement services.
procurement.Notes & relevance to digital health: TIER 3. Part of NHS PiP.

procurement.Source: East of England NHS Collaborative Procurement Hub
procurement.What it covers: Collaborative NHS frameworks; runs its own e-tendering portal.
procurement.Notes & relevance to digital health: TIER 3. Part of NHS PiP.

procurement.What it covers: Collaborative NHS frameworks and procurement services.
procurement.URL: Search 'NHS Commercial Solutions', verify current URL
procurement.Notes & relevance to digital health: Fourth member of NHS Procurement in Partnership (PiP).

procurement.What it covers: NHS-accessible framework agreements via an aligned membership / group purchasing model.
procurement.Notes & relevance to digital health: Private group purchasing organisation, an additional framework route alongside the NHS hubs.

procurement.Geographic scope: Scotland (incl. NHS Scotland boards + National Procurement)
procurement.What it covers: All Scottish public sector contract opportunities, including NHS National Services Scotland / National Procurement.
procurement.Notes & relevance to digital health: CPV-code email alerts. PCS-Tender portal for bid submission. Some data feeds available, worth investigating for automation.

procurement.What it covers: All Welsh public sector contract opportunities, including NHS Wales Shared Services Partnership.
procurement.Notes & relevance to digital health: CPV-code email alerts.

procurement.What it covers: Northern Ireland public sector tenders above thresholds, including Health & Social Care via BSO Procurement and Logistics Service.
procurement.Notes & relevance to digital health: CPV-code alerts.

procurement.Owner / operator: NHS England / Health Innovation Network (12 support orgs)
procurement.What it covers: Free centralised 'front door' that matches healthcare innovations to relevant support organisations.
procurement.Login to view?: Free account to submit an innovation
procurement.Notes & relevance to digital health: Not a live-opportunities portal, the navigation/support layer. Useful editorial reference and a signposting partner.

procurement.What it covers: From April 2026 NICE expands technology appraisals to cover digital health technologies, putting digital tools on a legal par with medicines for reimbursement.
procurement.Notes & relevance to digital health: Major NEW route to watch, national reimbursement pathway for digital health. Track evaluation calls and guidance.

procurement.What it covers: Regional innovation support, Innovation Exchange calls, and the NHS Innovation Accelerator.
procurement.Notes & relevance to digital health: Regional adoption support and occasional funded programmes, overlaps with the Grants tab.

procurement.What it covers: Umbrella body coordinating innovation adoption programmes across the NHS.
procurement.Notes & relevance to digital health: Strategic signposting layer, useful for understanding which innovation programmes are live.

procurement.Source: Tech Innovation Framework / GP IT Futures (Digital Care Services)
procurement.What it covers: Assured core GP IT systems and digital products for primary care, bought via the Digital Care Services catalogue. Tech Innovation Framework is the successor to GP IT Futures.
procurement.Notes & relevance to digital health: Essential route for anything touching primary care. GP IT Futures continuity contracts run to June 2026; TIF products (cloud, browser-based, open-API) coming on stream from April 2026.

procurement.What it covers: Mandates NHS commissioners and providers to fund a defined list of NICE-approved cost-saving devices, diagnostics and digital products.
procurement.Notes & relevance to digital health: Not a portal, a route to guaranteed local funding. Eligibility: positive NICE guidance, net saving within 3 years, budget impact under £20m/year. Health Innovation Networks support adoption.

procurement.What it covers: Cross-government technology frameworks beyond G-Cloud, e.g. Digital Specialists and Programmes, Vertical Application Solutions, software resale.
procurement.Notes & relevance to digital health: Additional CCS routes relevant to digital health builds and the services around them. Track the framework pipeline.

procurement.Source: Trust / ICB e-sourcing platforms (ProContract, In-tend, Delta eSourcing, Jaggaer)
procurement.What it covers: Sub-threshold and local opportunities published by individual trusts and ICBs not fully migrated to Atamis.
procurement.Notes & relevance to digital health: TIER 3. Declining as Atamis consolidates NHS procurement, but still carries local opportunities. Hard to monitor centrally, editorial/assisted tier.

procurement.Source: Devolved NHS national procurement bodies (NSS, NWSSP, BSO PaLS)
procurement.Owner / operator: NHS National Services Scotland; NHS Wales Shared Services Partnership; HSC Business Services Organisation
procurement.What it covers: National procurement arms of NHS Scotland, NHS Wales and HSC Northern Ireland, run national contracts and frameworks for their nations.
procurement.Notes & relevance to digital health: Tenders themselves are published on the devolved portals (PCS, Sell2Wales, eTendersNI). Listed here so the buyer side is visible alongside the portals.

grants.Owner: NHS England (delivered via Health Innovation Network)
grants.What it funds: Themed competitions for innovations addressing unmet NHS need, incl. digital health & AI; plus Investment Readiness support.
grants.Notes & relevance to digital health: TIER 2. Flagship NHS-aligned non-dilutive funding. Open to digital, AI, devices, service improvements.

grants.Owner: National Institute for Health and Care Research
grants.What it funds: Translational funding for medical devices, IVDs and digital health technologies, Product Development Awards, i4i Connect, Challenge Awards.
grants.Typical award size: Varies by stream; SMEs up to 100% funded
grants.Notes & relevance to digital health: TIER 2. Bridges proof-of-concept to real-world clinical adoption.

grants.What it funds: Multiple routes incl. AI in Health and Care Award, HealthTech Research Centres, EME programme.
grants.Notes & relevance to digital health: AI Award rounds run in waves; check current status. HRCs offer co-development/evaluation access.

grants.What it funds: Smart Grants, Biomedical Catalyst, sector competitions; plus Innovate UK Business Growth advisory support.
grants.Typical award size: Biomedical Catalyst up to ~£2m for large projects
grants.Notes & relevance to digital health: TIER 2. Core non-dilutive funder for UK healthtech R&D.

grants.What it funds: The portal where Innovate UK competitions are listed and applications submitted.
grants.Notes & relevance to digital health: TIER 2. Semi-automatable, competition search can be polled for new health/digital calls.

grants.Owner: UK Research and Innovation / Medical Research Council
grants.What it funds: Translational and developmental pathway schemes; research-oriented funding.
grants.Notes & relevance to digital health: More research/evidence-generation oriented, relevant for building the clinical evidence base.

grants.What it funds: Central cross-government grant finder, broad, not health-specific.
grants.Notes & relevance to digital health: TIER 2. Semi-automatable. Filter for health/digital relevance, high noise.

grants.What it funds: Regional innovation calls, Innovation Exchange, NHS Innovation Accelerator and signposting to national funding.
grants.Notes & relevance to digital health: Fragmented across 15 networks, an editorial aggregation opportunity in itself.

grants.What it funds: Funding and in-kind support for innovators to co-develop and evaluate health technologies.
grants.Notes & relevance to digital health: Access route to NHS evaluation partners as much as a cash grant.

grants.What it funds: Health research and translational funding, including some digital/data health.
grants.Notes & relevance to digital health: Less NHS-procurement-aligned; relevant for early research-stage digital health.

grants.Source: Scottish Enterprise / Welsh Government / Invest NI
grants.What it funds: R&D grants and SMART-type awards for companies based in the devolved nations.
grants.Notes & relevance to digital health: Eligibility tied to company location, worth a dedicated 'by nation' filter on the site.

grants.Source: Condition-specific charity funders (BHF, Cancer Research UK, etc.)
grants.What it funds: Innovation and translational awards tied to specific disease areas.
grants.Notes & relevance to digital health: Relevant only where the product has a disease focus, a 'by condition' tag would surface these well.

grants.What it funds: Challenge-led programmes, accelerator places and occasional funded cohorts.
grants.Notes & relevance to digital health: More support-in-kind than grant cash, but worth monitoring for funded calls.

grants.Source: NHS England transformation / innovation funding pots
grants.What it funds: Episodic funding pots tied to policy, e.g. AI/diagnostic funds, 10 Year Health Plan-linked allocations.
grants.Notes & relevance to digital health: Hardest to track and highest editorial value, exactly the 'stealth' problem from the procurement side.

grants.Source: Horizon Europe - Health Cluster (Cluster 1) & EIC Accelerator
grants.What it funds: Collaborative health research & innovation (Cluster 1) and deep-tech SME/startup funding (EIC Accelerator). UK orgs are eligible; Cluster 1 needs a consortium of 3+ countries.
grants.Typical award size: Cluster 1 up to ~EUR 90m per call; EIC Accelerator up to ~EUR 2.5m grant + equity
grants.Cadence: Annual work programmes; EIC has ~6 cut-offs/year
grants.Notes & relevance to digital health: Major route since UK association. EIC Accelerator cut-offs are predictable and monitorable. Innovate UK Business Connect signposts UK applicants.

grants.What it funds: Innovation and improvement programmes, e.g. Tech for Better Care (technology-enabled care at home), plus research and improvement grants.
grants.Typical award size: Programme-dependent (e.g. Tech for Better Care ~£2m programme)
grants.Notes & relevance to digital health: Increasingly focused on technology and AI in care, good fit for digital health with a service-improvement angle.

grants.What it funds: Translational funding and partnerships for therapeutics, diagnostics and medtech; LifeArc Ventures provides (dilutive) seed-Series B investment.
grants.Notes & relevance to digital health: Strongest for diagnostics / medtech translation. Note: LifeArc Ventures is equity, not non-dilutive.

grants.What it funds: Life sciences sector funds and programmes, e.g. innovative manufacturing funds and sector growth programmes.
grants.Notes & relevance to digital health: Policy-driven pots, high editorial value, hard to track via a feed.

grants.Source: Regional & Combined Authority innovation funds + UK Shared Prosperity Fund
grants.What it funds: Regional health innovation funds and economic development grants run by Combined Authorities (e.g. Greater Manchester, West Midlands) and the UK Shared Prosperity Fund.
grants.Notes & relevance to digital health: Eligibility tied to company location / region. Fragmented, a 'by region' filter on the site would surface these.
